text: The paper presents a thorough comparative study of DeepSeek-R1 and the widely used ChatGPT from OpenAI. It highlights their structural, design and operational differences and argues that the choice between them or future variants depends on the concrete needs of the organizations that will eventually adopt them. While ChatGPT leverages the same proprietary technology as other models from OpenAI such as the dense transformer and the supervised fine-tuned (SFT) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) that enhance its language abilities, DeepSeek-R1 leverages a mixture of experts architecture and multi-head latent attention (MLA) to achieve an optimal level of performance at a reasonable computational cost. The multi-stage training mechanism and the prevalence of the rule-based reinforcement learning in each stage give DeepSeek-R1 an extra bonus for specialized reasoning, math and technology tasks. However, for everyday conversation, natural language understanding and creativeness, ChatGPT is a more competitive model. We test both models in various aspects including performances, explainability, ethical and data protection. Our overall analysis sums up the pros and cons of using an open-source model as DeepSeek-R1 or a commercial such as ChatGPT. The conclusion remarks on the future AI challenges related to data quality, black boxes and legislation.
